name: Creamy Carmelized Garlic Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400°F.
2. Place an 8x10-inch piece of foil coated with 1 teaspoon Canola oil on a baking sheet. Arrange the garlic halves, cut side down, side by side. Fold up and wrap the foil around the garlic.
3. Bake for 40 minutes. Let cool for 10 minutes before opening the foil and transferring the garlic pulp to a food processor bowl.
4. Add vinegar, tarragon, and black pepper to the food processor. Process for 4-5 seconds, scraping once.
5. Add yogurt and process until combined.
6. Add the remaining oil and process until fully blended.
7. Refrigerate, tightly covered, up to one week. Serve with baguette slices or vegetable dippers.
Per serving: 2 tablespoons
92 calories, 2.5g (24%) fat, 0.7g fiber, 3.6g protein, 14.5g carbohydrates, 0mg cholesterol, 118mg sodium.
